This geometric heat equation describes how curvature is redistributed over time. The Ricci tensor \(\mathrm{Ric}\) itself is derived from the Riemann curvature tensor and encapsulates the average curvature in a given direction. Recent research explores the interplay between Ricci curvature bounds and the existence, compactness, and regularity of minimal surfaces in three-dimensional manifolds, often leveraging tools from geometric analysis and partial differential equations.
